title: Math — Circles
description: SAT-style circle questions with clean MC structure and rich solutions.

questions.prompt: A circle has center (−1, 1). A line is tangent to the circle at the point (5, −4). Which of the following points lies on the tangent line?

solution.approach: Find the slope of the radius, then take the negative reciprocal for the tangent.
steps: Slope of radius from (−1,1) to (5,−4) is −5/6.
Tangent slope is the negative reciprocal: 6/5.
Check which option forms slope 6/5 with (5,−4).
solution.takeaway: A tangent line is perpendicular to the radius at the point of tangency.

questions.prompt: The length of arc ADC is 5π and its central angle is 100°. What is the length of the remaining arc of the circle?

solution.approach: Arc length scales with central angle.
solution.takeaway: Use proportional reasoning with central angles.

questions.prompt: The equation x² + x + y² + y = 199/2 represents a circle. What is the radius of the circle?

solution.approach: Complete the square in both variables.
solution.takeaway: After completing the square, the RHS equals r².

questions.prompt: The equation (x + 4)² + (y − 19)² = 121 represents a circle. Which value could be the x-coordinate of a point on the circle?

solution.approach: Use center ± radius to find the allowable x-range.
steps: Center x is −4 and radius is 11.
solution.takeaway: Coordinates on a circle stay within one radius of the center.

questions.prompt: What is the center of the circle defined by x² + 20x + y² + 16y = −20?

solution.approach: Complete the square for x and y.
solution.takeaway: Center comes from the constants inside squared terms.

questions.prompt: A circle has radius 18. If the central angle of an arc is 120°, what is the arc length?

solution.approach: Convert degrees to radians and use s = rθ.
solution.takeaway: Always convert degrees before using s = rθ.

questions.prompt: A circle has diameter endpoints (2,4) and (2,14). What is the radius of the circle?

solution.approach: Find the midpoint and distance to an endpoint.
solution.takeaway: Radius is half the distance between diameter endpoints.

questions.prompt: Circle A has equation (x + 5)² + (y − 5)² = 4. Circle B has the same center and twice the radius. What is the value of k in Circle B’s equation?

solution.takeaway: Circle equations store r², not r.

questions.prompt: What is the diameter of the circle (x − 5)² + (y − 3)² = 16?

solution.approach: Take the square root to get radius, then double.

questions.prompt: The measure of arc RS is 100°. What is the measure of its central angle?

steps: So angle measure equals arc measure.
